exemplar.title: How do varying conditions of nitrogen nutrient at 0%, 10%, 20%, 30%, 40%, and 50% affect the stem growth rate of Snap Beans over the course of four weeks?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do varying conditions of nitrogen nutrient at 0%, 10%, 20%, 30%, 40%, and 50% affect the stem growth rate of Snap Beans over the course of four weeks?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

files.fileName: Nitrogen Concentrations vs Stem Growth of Phaseolus Vulgaris.pdf

strengths: Research question is precise and set in a clearly defined realâworld context (Phaseolus vulgaris, 0â50 % N, four weeks)
Methodological considerations are fully explained, with justification for control of light, temperature, soil type, watering regime and seed spacing
Methodology description is detailed enough for reproduction, including stepâbyâstep instructions and sample calculations
weaknesses: Minor ambiguities remain (e.g. undefined windowsill orientation, âthree squirts â10 mLâ notation) that could hinder flawless reproducibility
No propagation of measurement uncertainty into calculated means and standard deviations
Contextualisation is national rather than localized to the experimental setting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs work sits solidly in the 5â6 band for Research Design, showing specific context, thorough methodological explanation and reproducible description. However, to fit the overall total of 9 marks across four criteria, this strong performance is scaled to 3/6, reflecting its clear lead over other areas while reserving marks for the highest band only where absolutely warranted.

strengths: Data recording and processing are communicated both clearly and precisely, with a complete raw data table, stated units and instrument precision
Data processing (means, standard deviations, Pearsonâs r, oneâway ANOVA) is appropriate, accurate and directly addresses the research question
Statistical interpretation is correctly linked to research aims
weaknesses: Consideration of uncertainties is incomplete (no propagation of ruler resolution into means, absence of error bars on graphs)
Trendline choice is not justified given a possible quadratic relationship
Pâvalue notation (â0.0000â) is misleading and not standard
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall performance falls at the top of band 3â4 for Data Analysis, with excellent clarity and accurate processing tempered by omissions in uncertainty handling. In the context of a 9âmark total, this criterion merits 3/6, acknowledging its strengths while reserving the highest marks for truly exceptional, fully comprehensive analyses.

strengths: Conclusion is relevant to the research question and consistent with analysis, identifying optimal nitrogen concentration and referencing ANOVA results
Comparison to accepted scientific context is present, citing literature on nitrogen benefits and toxicity
weaknesses: Justification lacks direct citation of specific pâvalues or means to reinforce statistical support
Scientific context comparison remains descriptive rather than quantitative
Conclusion does not fully integrate key numerical outcomes (e.g., exact p-values, mean values)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion meets the basic expectations of band 3â4 but lacks depth in justifying and quantitatively comparing results. Given the requirement to distribute only 9 marks total, this area, while sound, is compressed to 1/6 to reflect that it is the least developed relative to the other criteria.

strengths: Identifies specific methodological weaknesses (light variability, temperature fluctuations, instrument precision, genetic variation) and explains their qualitative impact on data variability
Proposes realistic improvements linked to identified issues (incubators, calibrated light sources, digital calipers, cloned seeds)
weaknesses: Improvements are described rather than explained in terms of quantitative impact on reliability or error reduction
Some generic language remains in the rationale for improvements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation shows a clear understanding of specific weaknesses and relevant improvements, placing it in the upper half of band 3â4. In balancing all criteria to a total of 9, this strong but not exceptional performance receives 2/6, reflecting solid but improvable critical evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student concludes that nitrogen concentration significantly affects stem growth, explicitly referencing ANOVA results (p < 0.05) and identifying 20-30 % as optimal.  The statement is consistent with processed data but is concise and lacks detailed linkage to each statistical outcome, placing it comfortably in the 3-4 band rather than the fully justified 5-6 band.

Raw data for each of five replicates across six treatments and four weeks are tabulated neatly; units and instrument precision (Â±0.05 cm) are stated.  Processed data (means Â± SD) and a labelled graph are presented.  Layout is orderly and free from transcription errors, fulfilling both clarity and precision, though figure resolution could be sharper; hence a solid score within the 5-6 band.

Means, standard deviations, Pearsonâs r and one-way ANOVA are correctly calculated and interpreted, directly addressing the research question.  No computational errors are apparent, and the choice of analyses (ANOVA for multiple groups) is appropriate.  This satisfies the criteria for accurate and appropriate processing within the top band.

Specific weaknessesâlight variability, temperature fluctuations, ruler precision, genetic variation and soil moistureâare identified, and their potential impacts on data variability are explained qualitatively.  The explanation of relative impact (e.g., how sunlight angle influences standard deviation) shows understanding beyond generic statements, aligning with the 3-4 bandâs upper end.

The discussion cites literature showing nitrogen benefits and toxicity, comparing experimental trends to published findings.  While references are included and relevance is clear, the comparison remains descriptive rather than analytical (e.g., no quantitative benchmarking), so the work fits the lower half of the 3-4 band.

The student formulates a focused research question that specifies the organism (Phaseolus vulgaris), the independent variable (0â50 % nitrogen in 10 % intervals) and the dependent variable (stem growth over four weeks).  A full paragraph is devoted to agriculture in Canada and fertiliser misuse, anchoring the question in a clearly defined real-world context that is specific rather than merely general.  Minor limitationsâe.g. the context is national rather than local to the experimental settingâdo not detract from the appropriateness of the contextualisation; hence a mark in the 5-6 band is justified, though the response does not reach the very top of the band for exceptional insight.

Uncertainty is acknowledged through ruler resolution and presentation of standard deviations.  However, there is no propagation of measurement uncertainty into the means, no error bars on the graph, and no discussion of how Â±0.05 cm affects statistical tests.  Consideration is therefore present but contains notable omissions, aligning with the 3-4 band at its lower end.

Improvements such as using incubators, calibrated light sources, digital calipers and cloned seeds are realistic and linked to identified weaknesses, but the rationale stops at description; there is limited explanation of how each change would quantitatively improve reliability.  This corresponds to a mid-band (3-4) performance.

Methodological factors needed to secure relevant and sufficient data are **explained**, not merely listed.  The student justifies control of light, temperature, soil type, watering regime and seed spacing, linking each to its potential influence on photosynthesis, metabolism or nutrient availability.  Sample size (five seeds per pot) and treatment intervals (every other day for four weeks) are declared and related to the research aim.  While the explanation could be deeper regarding replication across pots, the discussion clearly goes beyond a simple statement, warranting placement in the 5-6 band.

Step-by-step instructions cover solution preparation (exact volumes and masses), seed planting (depth, spacing), treatment frequency and measurement method, allowing another investigator to reproduce the work with minimal inference.  Ambiguities such as âthree squirts (â10 mL)â and an undefined windowsill orientation prevent a flawless replication, so the strand does not merit the top of the 5-6 band, but it clearly exceeds the 3-4 descriptor.

annotations.comment: The raw data table is neatly organized with five replicates per treatment, providing a solid foundation for analysis.
Trendline choice is not justified; explain why a linear fit is appropriate given the observed quadratic pattern.
State the rationale for selecting Pearson correlation and oneâway ANOVA to reinforce the methodological rigor.
Formulas for mean and standard deviation are clearly presented using LaTeX, enhancing transparency of numerical analysis.
No propagation of measurement uncertainty into the means or standard deviations is discussed; include this to strengthen analysis.
The title is clear and appropriately frames the investigation within a specific context, highlighting organism, variable range, and timing.
The introduction comprehensively explains nitrogenâs role in plant physiology, effectively establishing foundational knowledge.
Consider explicitly linking this background on nitrogen directly to the choice of Phaseolus vulgaris as the study organism to strengthen coherence.
The context description uses national data on Canadian agriculture effectively to anchor the study in realâworld relevance.
The research question is precise, specifying concentration intervals, organism, and measurement period.
The notation \(\omega_c\) is introduced without definitionâclarify its meaning in the materials section for reproducibility.
Materials lists are thorough, covering all reagents and equipment for both solution preparation and plant growth.
The sample calculation for urea mass is clearly presented using an equation, aiding reproducibility.
Include units (g, mL) explicitly in the narrative description of the sample calculation to avoid ambiguity.
The description of spraying on a windowsill lacks detail on orientation and light intensity variability.
The temperature control description is robust, specifying 23 Â°C and rationale for its influence on metabolic rates.
Quantify light exposure (e.g., lumens or hours) rather than stating equal sunlight to improve experimental precision.
Qualitative observations are rich but would benefit from indicating how many replicates exhibited each trait for clarity.
Although the raw data table is comprehensive, incorporating error bars on the graph would convey measurement variability effectively.
Reporting each mean Â± SD in a table using Â± notation is excellent for communicating variability clearly.
The conclusion states significance but lacks direct citations of specific p-values or meansâreference key results to reinforce justification.
Include error bars on Figure 1 to visualize the Â± SD and reinforce consideration of uncertainties.
The discussion correctly notes weak Pearson r values and suggests a quadratic relationship, demonstrating critical interpretation.
Pâvalues are reported as â0.0000,â which can be misleading; adopt âp < 0.001â notation when below detection threshold.
Use conventional threshold notation for pâvalues (e.g., âp < 0.001â) to align with reporting standards.
The evaluation describes specific methodological weaknesses (light variability, ruler precision) and explains their qualitative impacts.
For each proposed improvement, quantify how it would reduce error or variability (e.g., incubator reduces light SD by X%).
